title: Project Wrap-Up & Agent Run
concept: Running the agent on broken code scripts and watching it debug them autonomously. 'Mini Project: Debugging Agent is complete!'.

exercises.prompt: What do we need to verify before running our agent on broken code?

options.text: That it outputs its final answer

exercises.explain: The key is to verify that the agent outputs its final answer on success.

cards.title: Get Ready to Run!
cards.body: Now it’s time to run your debugging agent on broken code scripts. Let’s see how well it can diagnose and fix the problems!

cards.title: Debugging Agent
cards.body: A debugging agent is a program designed to identify and correct errors in code automatically.

cards.title: Think of a Mechanic!
cards.body: Just like a mechanic fixes cars by finding problems and making repairs, a debugging agent finds mistakes in code and fixes them.

cards.title: Running the Agent
cards.body: When you run the debugging agent, it examines the broken code line by line and suggests fixes, similar to a teacher grading a homework assignment.

cards.title: Myth: The Agent Needs Perfect Code
cards.body: Many believe that the agent can only work if the code is perfectly written. In reality, it can handle messy code and find the errors!

cards.title: Before and After Running the Agent
cards.body: Watch how the agent transforms broken code into working code!

exercises.prompt: What does the debugging agent do when it runs on broken code?

options.text: It just reports errors without fixing them
It identifies and suggests fixes for errors
It ignores the code and does nothing
It rewrites the entire code from scratch

exercises.explain: The agent's role is to identify and suggest fixes for any errors it finds in the code.

exercises.prompt: True or False: The debugging agent cannot learn from its mistakes.
exercises.explain: The debugging agent is designed to learn and improve its capabilities over time.

exercises.prompt: The debugging agent examines the ________ in the code to find issues.
exercises.sentence: The debugging agent examines the broken code in the code to find issues.
exercises.explain: The debugging agent specifically looks at the broken code to identify what needs to be fixed.

exercises.prompt: Which of these statements describes the debugging agent's function?

options.text: It deletes all fear of code errors.
It magically makes all code perfect without checking.
It checks code for errors and suggests fixes.

exercises.explain: Correct! The debugging agent's main task is to check code for errors and provide suggestions for fixing them.

exercises.prompt: What is the main goal of running the debugging agent on broken code?

options.text: To learn how to write perfect code without errors.
To find errors and provide solutions for them.
To memorize all the coding languages.

exercises.explain: The correct answer is that the main goal of running the debugging agent on broken code is to find errors and provide solutions for them. This process helps improve code quality and efficiency. Mini Project: Debugging Agent is complete!
